reasoning: Silhouette at 1x: the rounded-rectangle button shape is instantly legible as a UI button — soft corners, clean edge, good contrast against both the dark-grey and the warm-golden backgrounds. That part passes. Charm: the pixel-art treasure-chest + gem-row motif is warm and toy-like, consistent with Echoes-of-Wisdom aesthetics; the colour palette (amber, cyan, red gems) has personality and the chest reads 'adventure loot' at a glance when zoomed. However two things knock it to marginal. First, at true 1x the chest icon collapses to a ~5px blob — the silhouette is readable as 'something inside a button' but not as 'chest', so the personality is invisible at shipping size. Second, the interior is sparse: the chest sits small and low-left leaving a large empty white field, so the button reads visually lightweight / unfinished rather than 'sticker-quality' confident. On dark bg contrast is fine; on the golden-frame light bg the light interior still reads. Edge cleanliness at 4x is good — no fringing or muddy anti-alias. Summary: clear-but-underpowered-on-charm at 1x; charm present at 4x but icon needs to be larger / more centred to survive downscale. Marginal, borderline pass if icon scale is bumped ~1.5x.
